# Step 4 — Bookmarks List Endpoint + Model

> **Epic**: E1.3.5 | **Task**: T1.3.5.2.1.1
> **Depends on**: Step 1 (InstapaperClient must exist)
> **Parallelizable with**: Step 6 (library UI can stub provider)

Shared context: [E1.3.5 Instapaper Context](../instructions/e1.3.5-instapaper-context.instructions.md)

---

## Task

Add the `getBookmarks()` method to `InstapaperClient` and create the `InstapaperBookmark` data model. This is the data layer for displaying saved articles in the library screen.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

### 1. Create `lib/features/content/models/instapaper_bookmark.dart`

```dart
/// An Instapaper saved article (bookmark).
///
/// Mirrors the bookmark object from the Instapaper Full API.
/// See: https://www.instapaper.com/api/full
class InstapaperBookmark {
  const InstapaperBookmark({
    required this.bookmarkId,
    required this.url,
    required this.title,
    this.description = '',
    this.hash = '',
    this.progress = 0.0,
    this.progressTimestamp = 0,
    this.time = 0,
    this.starred = false,
    this.privateSource = '',
  });

  /// Unique bookmark identifier.
  final int bookmarkId;

  /// Original article URL.
  final String url;

  /// Article title.
  final String title;

  /// Brief description or summary.
  final String description;

  /// Hash of URL + title + description + progress (for sync).
  final String hash;

  /// Reading progress (0.0 to 1.0).
  final double progress;

  /// Unix timestamp of last progress update.
  final int progressTimestamp;

  /// Unix timestamp of when the bookmark was saved.
  final int time;

  /// Whether the bookmark is starred.
  final bool starred;

  /// Empty for public bookmarks; source label for private ones.
  final String privateSource;

  /// The domain portion of the URL for display.
  String get domain {
    try {
      return Uri.parse(url).host;
    } catch (_) {
      return url;
    }
  }

  /// Reading progress as a percentage string (e.g. "50%").
  String get progressLabel => '${(progress * 100).round()}%';

  /// Whether the user has started reading this bookmark.
  bool get hasProgress => progress > 0.0;

  /// Parse from Instapaper API JSON response.
  factory InstapaperBookmark.fromJson(Map<String, Object?> json) {
    return InstapaperBookmark(
      bookmarkId: json['bookmark_id'] as int,
      url: json['url'] as String? ?? '',
      title: json['title'] as String? ?? '',
      description: json['description'] as String? ?? '',
      hash: json['hash'] as String? ?? '',
      progress: (json['progress'] as num?)?.toDouble() ?? 0.0,
      progressTimestamp: json['progress_timestamp'] as int? ?? 0,
      time: json['time'] as int? ?? 0,
      starred: json['starred'] == '1' || json['starred'] == true,
      privateSource: json['private_source'] as String? ?? '',
    );
  }

  /// Serialize to JSON map.
  Map<String, Object?> toJson() => {
    'bookmark_id': bookmarkId,
    'url': url,
    'title': title,
    'description': description,
    'hash': hash,
    'progress': progress,
    'progress_timestamp': progressTimestamp,
    'time': time,
    'starred': starred ? '1' : '0',
    'private_source': privateSource,
  };
}
```

### 2. Modify `lib/features/content/services/instapaper_client.dart`

Add this method to `InstapaperClient`:

```dart
/// Fetch saved bookmarks from Instapaper.
///
/// [limit] controls page size (1–500, default 25).
/// [folderId] filters by folder: 'unread' (default), 'starred', 'archive',
/// or a numeric folder ID.
///
/// Throws [InstapaperApiException] on failure.
Future<List<InstapaperBookmark>> getBookmarks({
  int limit = 25,
  String folderId = 'unread',
}) async {
  final response = await _signedPost('/api/1/bookmarks/list', body: {
    'limit': limit.clamp(1, 500).toString(),
    'folder_id': folderId,
  });

  if (response.statusCode != 200) {
    final error = _parseError(response);
    throw error ?? InstapaperApiException(
      errorCode: response.statusCode,
      message: 'Failed to fetch bookmarks: HTTP ${response.statusCode}',
    );
  }

  // Response is {"user":{...},"bookmarks":[{...}],"highlights":[...]}
  final json = jsonDecode(response.body) as Map<String, Object?>;
  final bookmarksList = json['bookmarks'] as List? ?? [];

  return bookmarksList
      .cast<Map<String, Object?>>()
      .where((b) => b['type'] == 'bookmark')
      .map(InstapaperBookmark.fromJson)
      .toList();
}
```

Add the import at the top of `instapaper_client.dart`:

```dart
import 'package:speedy_boy/features/content/models/instapaper_bookmark.dart';
```

---

## Verification

```bash
dart analyze lib/features/content/services/instapaper_client.dart lib/features/content/models/instapaper_bookmark.dart
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `InstapaperBookmark` model with all fields from API bookmark object
- [ ] `fromJson` factory handles nullable fields with safe defaults
- [ ] `domain` getter extracts host from URL
- [ ] `progressLabel` getter formats progress as percentage
- [ ] `getBookmarks()` on `InstapaperClient` with `limit` and `folderId` params
- [ ] Response parsed from `{"bookmarks":[...]}` structure (not flat array)
- [ ] Only items with `type == 'bookmark'` included (API may return user/meta objects)
- [ ] Limit clamped to 1–500
- [ ] `dart analyze` reports zero issues
- [ ] All public symbols have dartdoc comments
